title: AI Engineer
jd_text:
AI Engineer (LLM & Application Development), full-time on-site role.
Experience Level: 4-8 Years.
Role Overview: Bridge AI research and practical, scalable software. Design, build, and deploy AI-powered applications with heavy focus on LLMs, prompt engineering, and robust Python backends.
Key Responsibilities: Design/build/optimize applications leveraging LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source models); implement RAG, agentic workflows, and fine-tuning; write clean, maintainable, scalable Python code for APIs/backend services; apply CI/CD, automated testing, Git, Docker/Kubernetes; optimize AI pipelines for latency, cost, throughput; integrate vector databases (Pinecone, Chroma, Milvus); partner with product managers, data scientists, front-end engineers.
Required Skills: 4-8 years professional software engineering, at least 2 years building/deploying AI/LLM-powered applications; mastery of Python (FastAPI, Flask, Django); LangChain, LlamaIndex, or AutoGen; strong data structures, system design, RESTful APIs, microservices; cloud deployment (AWS/GCP/Azure) and MLOps/LLMOps; Bachelor's or Master's in CS/Engineering.
Nice-to-Haves: React/TypeScript for prototyping; evaluation frameworks (Ragas, TruLens); open-source contributions.

title: Generative AI Engineer
jd_text:
GenAI Developer/Engineer. Total Experience: 10-15 years; AI-ML and GenAI Experience: 4-6 years.
Responsible for building, deploying, operating GenAI solutions using modern agentic architectures.
Core Responsibilities: Design/develop/deploy GenAI and ML solutions using Azure OpenAI, Azure ML, Azure AI Studio; build LLM-powered apps with prompt engineering, tool/function calling, RAG, agentic AI patterns; develop Agent-to-Agent (A2A) workflows; implement Model Context Protocol (MCP); apply MLOps/LLMOps (CI/CD for models/prompts, versioning, monitoring, evaluation, hallucination/drift detection); implement LLM security/guardrails (prompt injection prevention, data leakage protection, output moderation); fine-tune models for token economics, latency, cost; build secure APIs/microservices; mentor junior engineers.
Technology Stack: Python; Azure OpenAI/open-source/local LLMs; Azure ML, Databricks, AI Services; LangChain, LangGraph, Semantic Kernel; MCP, A2A, RAG variants; Docker, REST APIs, CI/CD; SQL RDBMS, vector databases; Neo4j, GraphDB.

title: Generative AI Engineer
jd_text:
Notice Period: Immediate/Serving. Experience: 3 to 5 Years. Location: Pune or Remote.
Job Summary: Deep expertise in fine-tuning and optimizing LLMs (GPT, DeepSeek, LLAMA, BERT, MistralAI, Hugging Face). Strong background in RAG, agentive AI frameworks (LangGraph), building scalable secure AI solutions for autonomous agents and enterprise applications.
Key Responsibilities: Fine-tune/optimize LLMs for specific use cases; design/implement advanced RAG pipelines; build/deploy autonomous AI agents using LangGraph; develop/integrate RESTful APIs; collaborate cross-functionally; evaluate cutting-edge AI tech; ensure model efficiency, scalability, security compliance.
Required Skills: Proven LLM experience and model fine-tuning; hands-on RAG and LangGraph or similar; Python, C#, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot; PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face ecosystem; AI model deployment and inference optimization; prompt engineering and performance tuning.

title: Generative AI Engineer
jd_text:
Job Title: GenAI Engineer. Experience: 4+ years. Location Options: Pune, Gurgaon, Noida, Bangalore.
Skills: Generative AI Concepts, Coding & Debugging, Cloud Deployment expertise, Soft skills/Communication.
Job Description: Work with client stakeholders to translate requirements into AI/GenAI-based solutions; design/develop/deploy Generative AI models leveraging LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face); build prompt frameworks, embeddings, RAG pipelines; integrate AI models with enterprise workflows/APIs; fine-tune/customize foundation models for client use cases; ensure model performance, scalability, cost efficiency; document solution designs; apply Python and GenAI frameworks and vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS); use cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), Git/GitHub, CI/CD.
Requirements added by poster: 2+ yrs LLM; 4+ yrs IT; 2+ yrs Generative AI; 3+ yrs Python.

title: GenAI Engineer (Agentic AI)
jd_text:
Role: Agentic AI / GenAI Engineer. Experience: 3 to 9 years. Job Type: Full Time.
What You'll Do: Design/develop agentic AI systems (single- and multi-agent workflows); implement planning, memory, tool-use patterns, human-in-the-loop; build/deploy/scale production-grade GenAI solutions; integrate AI agents with enterprise applications/APIs/data sources using secure governed access; implement RAG, enterprise knowledge grounding, vector search; contribute to LLMOps/AgentOps (evaluation, monitoring, debugging, optimization); ensure Responsible AI practices (security, access control, governance, auditability); support observability, performance tuning, cost optimization; mentor junior engineers.
Expertise: 3-9 years software/AI engineering; at least 1 year hands-on GenAI/LLM solutions; strong Python; agentic design patterns, tool-use, multi-agent orchestration; RAG pipelines, vector databases; LLMOps/AgentOps exposure; experience with Azure/AWS/Bedrock/GCP Vertex AI; open-source and proprietary LLMs.

title: AI / GenAI Engineer
jd_text:
Role Summary: AI/ML Engineer with strong Python skills, building production-ready AI systems including multi-agent systems and RAG pipelines, ensuring scalability, reliability, cost efficiency.
Key Responsibilities: Model integration (deploy/maintain ML models in production); agentic frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen) for multi-agent systems; RAG (hybrid search, reranking, semantic chunking) with Pinecone/Weaviate/Chroma; Model Context Protocol (MCP) familiarity; data pipeline support (clean/preprocess/augment datasets); prompt engineering; token optimization; MLOps basics (CI/CD, model versioning, automated prompt testing); code reviews, sprint planning, documentation; continuous learning.
Qualifications: Bachelor's in CS/Data Science or related; Python proficiency; basic ML concepts; RESTful APIs; Git; experimentation mindset; 5+ years relevant experience.

title: AI Engineer

title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer (GenAI & Agentic AI)
jd_text:
Role: AI/ML Engineer - Generative AI & Agentic AI. Experience: 1-4 Years. Location: Bangalore. Work Mode: WFO 5 Days. Employment Type: Permanent.

title: AI / ML Engineer

title: Machine Learning Engineer

title: GenAI Engineer (AgenticAI)

title: Applied AI Engineer II
jd_text:
About Learneo
Learneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal.

About Quillbot
Quillbot was founded in 2017 with a mission to help students and professionals—especially those learning English—strengthen their writing. Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Whether you're writing, designing, coding, or collaborating, Quillbot is a place where anyone can create at the speed of thought. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us.

Role Overview
We're looking for an Applied AI Engineer to join our MLOps team and take ownership of the infrastructure that keeps our machine learning models running reliably in production. This role is essential to maintaining the uptime and performance of our ML systems as usage scales. You'll work closely with data scientists, researchers, and software engineers to bridge the gap between experimentation and production—turning research artifacts into robust, monitored, and continuously improving services. This is a hands-on opportunity to shape our on-premises MLOps practices and improve engineering across the ML stack.

Responsibilities

Collaborate with experienced data scientists and software engineers to gain insights into building scalable and efficient data pipelines, model training, and deployment systems. 
Troubleshoot issues in the entire machine learning infrastructure, from Linux, Docker, and Kubernetes up to the highest levels of our ML stack. Resolve issues, improve system performance, and make our stack the best in the industry. 
Assist in the design and development of on-premises MLOps solutions to support the delivery of machine learning models, and a seamless handover between research and productionization of ML artifacts 
Drive and uphold high engineering standards, bringing consistency to codebases encountered and ensuring software is adequately reviewed, tested, and integrated. 
Optimize existing models for better performance and throughput. 
Incorporate ML model training, validation, and evaluation settings in addition to traditional coding tests     like unit and integration testing. 
Build and maintain tools for deployment, monitoring, and operations. - Continuously refine and enhance CI/CD workflows to support the evolving needs of the machine learning infrastructure. 
Ideal Candidate 

3+ years of experience in MLOps or full stack Machine Learning - Good programming skills in a modern programming language (Python, Scientific Python Stack, Cuda). 
Understanding of the MLOps life cycle and experience with MLOps workflows.
Experience with tools & practices of the trade, such as Kubernetes, GCP/AWS/Azure, CI/CD, common ML frameworks, and data management.
A keen interest in machine learning engineering and a willingness to explore how it can be scaled effectively. 
Strong desire to learn and good communication skills, with an enthusiasm for collaborative problem-solving.
Benefits & Perks

Competitive salary and annual bonus
Medical coverage
Life and accidental insurance
Vacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)
Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshops
Maternity & parental leave
Hybrid & remote model with flexible working hours
On-site & remote company events throughout the year
Tech & WFH stipends & new hire allowances
Employee referral program
Premium access to Quillbot
*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process. 

This role is eligible for hire in India.

 

We are a virtual-first company with employees across the United States, Canada, India, Germany, and the Netherlands. Our compensation approach is market-based and varies by location while ensuring fairness, consistency, and transparency.

The base pay for this role is determined by several factors, including:

Relevant professional experience
Skills and expertise
Scope and responsibilities of the role
Internal equity considerations 
In addition to base salary, total compensation may include bonus, equity, and benefits. Eligibility depends on role, level, and location.

We are committed to equal pay for equal work, and all compensation decisions are made using consistent, gender-neutral criteria in alignment with pay transparency requirements.

Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!
